notes: Examine.com evidence summary on cycling; covers cardiovascular, metabolic, and mortality outcomes
PMID identity unconfirmed from indirect search; likely cycling-mortality related study but content not verified — replaced with confirmed references below
Celis-Morales BMJ 2017; 263450 UK participants; cycling commuting associated with 41% lower all-cause mortality, 52% lower CVD incidence, 45% lower cancer mortality
Dose-response meta-analysis: 23% lower all-cause mortality at highest cycling level (RR 0.77); benefits present at any cycling volume
